commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Moreover you have taken your sons and your daughters, whom you have borne unto me, and these have you sacrificed unto them to be devoured. Is this act of your harlotries a small matter, Thy sons: so he calls them to show his indignation, though he acknowledges them for his, (ver. ) to enhance the crime. Who could have thought that such cruel sacrifices would ever take place. (4 Kings xxiii. ) (Calmet) Adulteresses bring in the children of others; but the Jews sacrifice their own to idols, 4 Kings xvi. (Worthington).
